rationale: The pair is not an obvious incidental mention: both papers focus on nonlinguistic product sound cues and consumer inferences, with the cited paper establishing core auditory meaning-transfer effects (pitch  size, with boundary conditions) and the citing paper extending auditory signaling to volume and status. Given this conceptual overlap in sensory/auditory marketing and plausible foundational use, it merits second-stage review despite limited raw reference context (DOI-only match).
